title: Diplazium thelypteroides
taxonomy: Diplazium thelypteroides belongs to the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns or marsh ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, one of the largest orders of ferns. The genus Diplazium is a type of fern that is typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, with D. thelypteroides being a specific species within this genus. The classification of D. thelypteroides can be further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Pteridophyta. The specific epithet "thelypteroides" suggests a resemblance to the genus Thelypteris.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its use in treating various health conditions.
